title: Introduction to ADNIMetabo
text: Alzheimer’s disease complex multifactorial neurodegenerative disorder affects millions people worldwide. Alzheimer’s Disease Neuroimaging Initiative (ADNI) multisite study launched 2003 collect share data can help researchers understand progression Alzheimer’s disease. ADNIMetabo package developed make ADNI datasets readily available easily accessible analysis. package, includes range datasets, focusing different aspects ADNI study: Demographic data: Participant demographic information age, sex, education, ethnicity, marital status. Clinical assessments: Baseline follow-clinical data, including diagnosis, CDR-SB, ADAS scores, MMSE, RAVLT scores. Biomarker data: Measurements CSF ABETA, TAU, PTAU, FDG-PET, PIB, AV45. Imaging data: MRI field strength, FreeSurfer software version, various brain region volumes. Cognitive domposite scores: ADNI modified Preclinical Alzheimer’s Cognitive Composite (PACC) scores Digit Symbol Substitution Trails B. ECog scores: ECog scores participants study partners. document walk steps install load ADNIMetabo package, access different datasets, provide examples explore analyze data. intended serve guide researchers, clinicians, students interested using ADNI datasets studies. hope using ADNIMetabo package, users can take advantage extensive well-curated datasets ADNI study advance understanding Alzheimer’s disease contribute development diagnostic therapeutic strategies. Let’s start get access various datasets ADNI:
code: devtools::install_github("danymukesha/ADNIData") #for installation library(ADNIMetabo)  data("demographic_data", package = "ADNIMetabo") data("baseline_clinical_data", package = "ADNIMetabo") data("follow_up_clinical_data", package = "ADNIMetabo") data("biomarker_data", package = "ADNIMetabo") data("imaging_data", package = "ADNIMetabo") data("cognitive_composite_data", package = "ADNIMetabo") data("ecog_scores_data", package = "ADNIMetabo")

title: Introduction to ADNIMetabo
text: example can explore demographic data:
code: head(demographic_data) #>   RID  AGE PTGENDER PTEDUCAT        PTETHCAT PTRACCAT PTMARRY #> 1   2 74.3     Male       16 Not Hisp/Latino    White Married #> 2   3 81.3     Male       18 Not Hisp/Latino    White Married #> 3   3 81.3     Male       18 Not Hisp/Latino    White Married #> 4   3 81.3     Male       18 Not Hisp/Latino    White Married #> 5   3 81.3     Male       18 Not Hisp/Latino    White Married #> 6   4 67.5     Male       10     Hisp/Latino    White Married

title: Introduction to ADNIMetabo
code: # summarize age and education summary(demographic_data$AGE) #>    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max.    NA's  #>   50.40   68.50   73.20   73.21   78.10   91.40       9 summary(demographic_data$PTEDUCAT) #>    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max.  #>    4.00   14.00   16.00   16.11   18.00   20.00  # count the number of participants by sex and ethnicity table(demographic_data$PTGENDER) #>  #> Female   Male  #>   7440   8981 table(demographic_data$PTETHCAT) #>  #>     Hisp/Latino Not Hisp/Latino         Unknown  #>             550           15798              73

title: Introduction to ADNIMetabo
code: # summarize baseline CDR-SB and MMSE scores summary(baseline_clinical_data$CDRSB_bl) #>    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max.  #>   0.000   0.000   0.500   1.215   2.000  10.000 summary(baseline_clinical_data$MMSE_bl) #>    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max.    NA's  #>   16.00   26.00   29.00   27.76   30.00   30.00       2  # plot the distribution of baseline ADAS 11 scores hist(baseline_clinical_data$ADAS11_bl, main = "Baseline ADAS 11 Scores", xlab = "ADAS 11 Score")

title: Introduction to ADNIMetabo
code: # summarize CSF ABETA and TAU levels summary(biomarker_data$ABETA) #>    Length     Class      Mode  #>     16421 character character summary(biomarker_data$TAU) #>    Length     Class      Mode  #>     16421 character character  # plot the relationship between CSF ABETA and TAU plot(biomarker_data$ABETA, biomarker_data$TAU, main = "CSF ABETA vs TAU", xlab = "CSF ABETA", ylab = "CSF TAU") #> Warning in xy.coords(x, y, xlabel, ylabel, log): NAs introduced by coercion #> Warning in xy.coords(x, y, xlabel, ylabel, log): NAs introduced by coercion

title: Introduction to ADNIMetabo
text: ADNIMetabo package provide set datasets ADNI study, making easier analyze understand progression Alzheimer’s disease. vignette shown load explore datasets, nd perform basic analyses. detailed documentation dataset, please refer respective help files:

title: Introduction to ADNIMetabo
text: data included package Alzheimer’s Disease Neuroimaging Initiative (ADNI) database. ADNI multisite study launched 2003 National Institute Aging (NIA), National Institute Biomedical Imaging Bioengineering (NIBIB), Food Drug Administration (FDA), private pharmaceutical companies, non-profit organizations.

what: Prepare and process ADNI metabolomics data
title: Prepare and process ADNI metabolomics data
text: ADNIMetabo now also provide collection Metabolomics datasets Alzheimer’s Disease Neuroimaging Initiative (ADNI).

title: Prepare and process ADNI metabolomics data
text: Demographic data: Participant demographic information age, sex, education, ethnicity, marital status. Baseline clinical data: Baseline clinical assessments including diagnosis, CDR-SB, ADAS scores, MMSE, RAVLT scores. Follow-clinical data: Follow-clinical assessments. Biomarker data: Biomarker measurements including CSF ABETA, TAU, PTAU, FDG-PET, PIB, AV45. Imaging data: Imaging measurements MRI field strength, FreeSurfer software version, various brain region volumes. Cognitive composite scores: Cognitive composite scores including mPACCdigit mPACCtrailsB. ECog scores: ECog scores participants study partners. Metabolomic data: [available soon]

title: Prepare and process ADNI metabolomics data
text: data included package Alzheimer’s Disease Neuroimaging Initiative (ADNI) database. ADNI multisite study launched 2003 National Institute Aging (NIA), National Institute Biomedical Imaging Bioengineering (NIBIB), Food Drug Administration (FDA), private pharmaceutical companies, non-profit organizations.
